name: cotton
info: Cotton, famously known as "white gold", is a major commercial crop requiring warm, humid conditions. Optimal farming requires Bt. seeds, proper spacing, targeted fertilization, and water management.Key Cultivation PracticesClimate: Thrives best in temperatures between 21� C and 37� C with 500 to 1100 mm of annual rainfall.Sowing Time: Typically sown at the start of the monsoon (June�July). For rain-fed or irrigated conditions, seeds are hand-dibbled or sown via drill.Crop ManagementFertilizer & Nutrition: Split Nitrogen applications (like Urea) alongside Phosphorus and Potash are recommended to avoid excess vegetative growth.Weed Control: Apply pre-emergence herbicides like Pendimethalin within 3 days of sowing to suppress early grass and broadleaf weeds.Water Requirement: Requires 700 to 1,200 mm of water, with peak demand during the flowering and boll-development stage

name: maize
info:
Maize, or corn, is a staple food for humans and a key ingredient in many industrial products. Here's some information about maize in India: 
 
Production
India is one of the top maize producers in the world, and is the 5th largest producer and 14th largest exporter. The top maize-producing states in India include Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar, Andhra Pradesh, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, and Telangana. 
 
Uses
Maize is used for many purposes, including: 
 
Food: A staple food for humans 
 
Feed: Used as feed for poultry and livestock 
 
Industry: Used as a raw material for thousands of industrial products, including starch, oil, protein, alcoholic beverages, food sweeteners, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, film, textiles, gum, packaging, and paper 
 
Contribution to the economy
Maize contributes nearly 8% to the national food basket and more than Rs. 100 billion to the agricultural GDP. It also provides employment to over 100 million man-days.

name: soybean
info:
Production
India is the world's fourth largest producer of soybeans by area, and fifth largest by total production. The major soybean producing states are Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Rajasthan, Karnataka, and Telangana. 
 
Cultivation
Soybeans are grown in a rainfed ecosystem with low and erratic rainfall. 
 
Productivity
The productivity of soybeans in India ranges from 400-2300 kg/ha, with a median of 1000 kg/ha. Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra contribute 88% of the country's total soybean production. 
 
Harvest
As of September 15, 2023, the area under soybean planting was 12.55 million hectares, up from 12.43 million hectares in 2022. However, the crop acreage in Madhya Pradesh dropped to 5.335 million hectares in 2023-24 from 5.387 million hectares in 2022-23. 
 
Soybean Processors Association of India
The Soybean Processors Association of India (SOPA) hosts the International Soy Conclave
